title: Flaky Buttery Indian Lamb Rogan Josh
description: A satvik-friendly, nostalgic Indian dish featuring ground nutmeg and nuka-pickle, this buttery and flaky lamb rogan josh is a classic Bengali recipe. It is made with tender lamb and a blend of spices.
instructions: Heat the melted butter in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the ground lamb and cook until it is browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the ground nutmeg, nuka-pickle, garam masala, cumin powder, coriander powder, and salt to the pan and stir to combine.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer the mixture for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Use a fork to flake the lamb into small pieces as it cooks.
Continue to simmer the mixture for another 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the lamb is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Serve the lamb rogan josh hot, garnished with fresh cilantro if desired.
Traditionally, this dish is served with basmati rice or naan bread.
To make it more authentic, use a mixture of spices and adjust to taste.
